title: B'rknaa
content:

{{Quote|Like most rock-based creatures, this species from the moon Indobok can be particularly inscrutable since their microscopic structures are so different from midi-chlorian-infused organic cells.|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] Bowspritz|<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>}}
The '''B'rknaa''' were a [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]]-based [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to [[Indobok]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In actuality, the moon was itself an enormous member of the species. The B'rknaa's true appearance was that of [[Crystal/Legends|crystalline]] clusters, but their inner energy could induce life into inanimate matter. When they were deemed old enough to take up a host body, the B'rknaa crystals animated giant stone husks in which they could move about. These sentients were considered unusual by [[Human/Legends|Humans]], and they were known to [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Researcher|biologists]] for their resistance to [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]]. The B'rknaa were also capable of merging together several stone bodies to form a much larger unit.

The B'rknaa of Indobok were known to the wider [[galactic community]] since at least the first [[Time/Legends|millennium]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Compared to the civilized worlds of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], their [[technology level]] was very low: they did not build [[City/Legends|cities]] and wore no [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], but they did develop a [[Language/Legends|language]] of their [[B'rknaa language|own]]. Despite their bulky appearance, they were usually friendly to strangers, with the notable exception of those who tried to steal B'rknaa crystals. Around [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]], the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Jace Forno]] planned on stealing some of them so she could supply the [[Smuggler/Legends|black market]]. Thanks to the intervention of the [[Pitareeze family]] from Kalarba, Forno's henchmen were [[Skirmish on Indobok|driven off]], and her plan was thwarted. [[26 ABY/Legends|Twenty]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Great War]], the B'rknaa were put in jeopardy when the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], a species of [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders, [[Battle of Kalarba|destroyed]] Kalarba, dispersing [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] material across the [[Kalarba system/Legends|system]] in the process.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|It's rather difficult to explain in Basic, but these crystals are the B'rknaa, or at least will be when they are old enough.|C-3PO|<ref name="SWDK4" />}}
[[File:BrknaaCrystal-SWDTKA4.png|thumb|left|A B'rknaa crystal cluster]]
The B'rknaa hailed from [[Indobok]], a heavily cratered [[Moon/Legends|moon]] orbiting the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]], near [[Hosk Station/Legends|Hosk Station]]<ref name="SWDK4" /> in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> In their purest form, the B'rknaa appeared as [[Color/Legends|blueish]], irregularly faceted and roughly cylindrical [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]], which gathered in clusters on the surface of Indobok. Their crystalline nature made their biology similar<ref name="SWDK4" /> to that of the [[Shard (species)|Shards]] from the planet [[Orax]]<ref name="AE">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> and the [[Tsil]] of the planet [[Nam Chorios/Legends|Nam Chorios]].<ref name="EGAS">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref>

The B'rknaa prisms were able to channel vast amounts of complex energy; in fact, one large B'rknaa specimen was enough to handle all the systems power of a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]]. B'rknaa had the power to animate inert materials, which they then used as host bodies. When they were considered old enough,<ref name="SWDK4" /> a B'rknaa took control of an enormous, bulky form made of rock—the only substance available in sufficient quantities on the moon.<ref name="KAL1-2" /> In fact, the moon of Indobok itself was a single, massive, adult B'rknaa. All other B'rknaa on its surface were its "offspring,"<ref name="SWDK4" /> in that all B'rknaa were controlled by one [[Hive mind/Legends|shared mind]].<ref name="KAL1-2" /> Brown or gray, their giant, bulky host shells appeared [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] in shape, with two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and two arms ending with four-fingered hands. Their legs were roughly shaped like pillars, and their feet were almost round. A typical B'rknaa body featured an ashen, expressionless face with a gaping [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] and two forward-facing, white [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Several units were able to join their bodies to form larger and more threatening beings.<ref name="SWDK4" /> The stone beings from Indobok were also immune to every direct [[Force power/Legends|application]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] thanks to their [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]]-based structure and their hive mind. [[Scientist/Legends|Scientists]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] theorized that the Force itself allowed certain [[Species/Legends|species]] to develop barriers that could redirect its flow.<ref name="TJP" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Yes. These charming creatures you see around us are actually quite gentle.|C-3PO|<ref name="SWDK4" />}}
[[File:Brknaa-SWDTKA4.jpg|thumb|right|180px|A B'rknaa husk being animated]]
On Indobok, the B'rknaa who took stone bodies led a simple lifestyle,<ref name="SWDK4" /> considered archaic by [[Technology level|galactic standards]].<ref name="SWDK4" /><ref name="AE" /> They did not build [[City/Legends|cities]] or communal habitats, living in the open [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]], and they wore no [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]]. Despite their brutish and frightening appearance, the B'rknaa were indeed [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]]. They usually were peaceful and welcoming, only showing signs of aggression toward those who wanted to steal their precious crystals—which were not mere commodities, but were in fact [[Youngling/Legends|younger]], unshelled B'rknaa.<ref name="SWDK4" />

When they were considered old enough, the B'rknaa prisms were given a humanoid stone husk that was animated simply by putting the crystal cluster inside it. While moving around in their stone shells, B'rknaa were capable of speech. [[B'rknaa language|Their language]] consisted of creaking and cracking sounds. Although it was possible, at least for a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], to learn the basics of the B'rknaa [[Language/Legends|language]] in a few [[Standard day/Legends|days]], some of its concepts were difficult to translate to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="SWDK4" />

Like the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]],<ref name="TNEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> the [[Taurill/Legends|Taurill]],<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], part VII {{C|Under ''The Darksaber Threat''}}</ref> and many other species, the B'rknaa didn't recognize individuality, due to their shared mind. Indeed, there was no first person personal pronoun in their language. When a single stone giant spoke, it voiced the opinion of the whole species.<ref name="SWDK4" />

==History==
{{Quote|I don't understand… Living stone? How is this possible?|Baron Pitareeze|<ref name="SWDK4" />}}
[[File:Brknaa_by_Bowspritz.png|thumb|left|upright|A B'rknaa stone giant drawn by Master Bowspritz]]
The crystalline B'rknaa and their rock bodies were known to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Order by at least [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. The Jedi noted that the B'rknaa were difficult to affect with mind-based Force powers, and the rock beings were resistant to all direct applications of the Force. Following the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]], the [[Jenet/Legends|Jenet]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] of the Order, [[Fae Coven]], created ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''.<ref name="TJP" /> In this guidebook, the [[Tynnan/Legends|Tynnan]]<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/31329847|board=Literature|thread=The Jedi Path. (Now you too can actually pretend with slightly more accuracy that you're a Jedi!)|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan_Wallace]]|date=2010-10-25|time=1:50 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180609035645/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-jedi-path-now-you-too-can-actually-pretend-with-slightly-more-accuracy-that-youre-a-jedi.31091974/page-9}}</ref> [[Jedi Biologist|biologist]] [[Bowspritz]] mentioned the B'rknaa in a chapter entitled "Advanced Cultures and Politics in the Republic," listing them among those species resistant to the Force.<ref name="TJP" />

Outside [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] circles, the stone giants of Indobok remained largely unknown, unlike other mineral sentients such as the [[stone eel]]s of [[Storthus]]. Even the [[Pitareeze family|Pitareezes]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] family from Kalarba, did not know of the species before they encountered mobile B'rknaa in [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]]. That year, the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Scoundrel/Legends|thief]] [[Jace Forno]] intended to visit Indobok and steal a bag of crystals, hoping she could sell them on the [[Smuggler/Legends|black market]]. Forno rented two [[Droid/Legends|droids]] from the Pitareezes—the protocol droid [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—so they could accompany her to the cratered moon. The Corellian [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] ordered the droids to find crystal clusters and take as many as they could while she took care of the stone giants. C-3PO successfully stole a single large crystal, but several B'rknaa in stone form thereafter attacked Forno and the droids. The party then ran to their [[Starship/Legends|starship]] for safety, but C-3PO could not make it in [[Time/Legends|time]] because his joints froze. Forno abandoned the gold-colored protocol droid on Indobok, intending to come back later with more men and weapons.<ref name="SWDK4" />

[[File:BrknaaCombined-Droids1994-4.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Forno's thieves could not match the strength of the B'rknaa united in a single body.]]
While abandoned on the moon, C-3PO discovered the truth about the B'rknaa's gentle nature and their sentient crystals. Eventually, the [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Pitareeze]], headed for Indobok—along with his son [[Jarth Pitareeze|Jarth]], his daughter-in-law [[Meg Pitareeze|Meg]], and his grandson [[Nak Pitareeze|Nak]]—to rescue C-3PO. Much to their amazement, the Kalarbans made their first contact with the B'rknaa. Through C-3PO, the Humans befriended the B'rknaa and spent the night on the moon. Suddenly, Forno and her minions landed on Indobok, ready to do battle against the stone giants. The B'rknaa put up a [[Skirmish on Indobok|valiant defense]] with the help of their new Pitareeze allies. The stone giants fought their enemies with their fists, eventually merging together into a single, colossal entity. The united B'rknaa shattered one of Forno's armed vehicle on the ground. The Corellian thief's henchmen were no match for the mineral titan, and they beat a hasty retreat. The precious B'rknaa crystals were thus restored to safety.<ref name="SWDK4" />

Nearly twenty years later, in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], a species of technophobic [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaders]] from [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|another galaxy]], endangered the B'rknaa.<ref name="BP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''</ref> In an [[Battle of Kalarba|assault]] against Kalarba and its moon, the Vong used [[Yo'gand's Core]], a battle tactic that they had [[Destruction of Sernpidal|employed]] earlier on the moon of [[Sernpidal/Legends|Sernpidal]]. Hosk Station, Kalarba's other moon, was drawn in, and both the planet and its station were destroyed in the process.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> As a consequence of the impact, [[Radiation/Legends|radioactive]] material was spread across the whole [[Kalarba system/Legends|Kalarba system]], rendering it uninhabitable for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]].<ref name="NJOSB">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref>

==B'rknaa in the galaxy==
{{Quote|It'll be a fortune on the open market!|Jace Forno|<ref name="SWDK4" />}}
Because of their ability to channel energy, B'rknaa crystals were considered precious commodities by the few off-worlders who knew of their existence. The thief Jace Forno once sold a single crystal for fifty thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. She intended to steal as many clusters as she could and sell them on the open market in order to gain substantial income.<ref name="SWDK4" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|P. 86: Here's a quick overview of the galaxy's most important species circa 1000 BBY. I like the stylized portraits, particularly the Squib, which were done by Derek Thompson.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="Endnote">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-3.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 3 of 4)|date=2010-12-08}}</ref>}}
[[File:Swdr4.jpg|thumb|upright|The B'rknaa debuted in ''Star Wars: Droids'' (1994) 4.]]
The B'rknaa first appeared in [[July]] [[1994]], in the [[Droids (1994) 4|fourth issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Droids (1994)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The story was written by [[Dan Thorsland]] and pencilled by [[Bill Hughes]].<ref name="SWDK4" /> As Indobok itself is a member of the species, the list of appearances below includes the works in which the moon was featured.

After a sixteen-year hiatus, the B'rknaa were mentioned in [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']], a reference book written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and published on [[September 28]], [[2010]]. The species had its own entry in a guide to important or unusual [[Alien/Legends|alien]] races, complete with a stylized illustration by [[Derek Thompson]]. In the book, B'rknaa were described as "rock-based creatures."<ref name="TJP" /> This description apparently refers to their mobile bodies, since the beings themselves are crystalline in nature. The words "rock" and "crystal" cannot be used interchangeably. A [[Wikipedia:Crystal|crystal]] is a pure [[Wikipedia:Mineral|mineral]], meaning it has an exact chemical composition, while a [[Wikipedia:Rock (geology)|rock]] is an aggregate of two or more minerals.<ref name="Crystal vs rock">{{WebCite|url=http://www.madsci.org/posts/archives/2000-08/966273615.Es.r.html|text=Is crystal a rock?|work=MadSci Network|archivedate=20160830145442}}</ref>
